faqs.title: Am I eligible to apply for a Canada work permit extension?
faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria to apply for a work permit extension in Canada?
faqs.answer: You can generally apply for a Canada work permit extension if you still meet the conditions of your original work permit, your passport is valid for the new requested period, and you apply before your current permit expires. You may also need a new Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA) or proof of continued LMIA-exempt status, depending on your category.
faqs.preview_text: Learn if you qualify to extend your existing Canadian work permit before it expires.

faqs.title: What happens if I apply for a Canada work permit extension after expiry?
faqs.question: Is there a grace period if I miss the expiry date of my Canadian work permit when applying for an extension?
faqs.answer: If you apply for an extension after your work permit expires, you may lose your maintained status and be considered out of status. In some cases, you may be able to apply for restoration of status within 90 days, but you generally cannot work during this period until a new permit is issued.
faqs.preview_text: Understand your options if you missed the deadline to file your work permit extension in Canada.

faqs.title: How long does it take to process a Canada work permit extension?
faqs.question: What are the typical processing times for in-Canada work permit extension applications?
faqs.answer: Processing times vary by category and workload. In many cases, in-Canada work permit extension applications take several weeks to a few months. Check the latest IRCC processing time tool for up-to-date estimates.
faqs.preview_text: Typical timelines for in-Canada work permit extension processing.

faqs.title: What is a competitive CRS score for Express Entry?
faqs.question: What Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score should I aim for to receive an Invitation to Apply through Express Entry?
faqs.answer: A competitive CRS score changes over time with each draw. Historically, scores in the high 400s or above have often been competitive for Federal Skilled Worker and Canadian Experience Class draws, but you should monitor recent IRCC draw results to understand current trends.
faqs.preview_text: Guidance on what CRS score might be considered competitive in the Express Entry pool.

faqs.title: How does the Australia skilled visa points test work?
faqs.question: What factors are counted in the points test for Australian skilled migration visas?
faqs.answer: Australia's skilled visa points test considers your age, English language ability, skilled employment experience, qualifications, Australian study, partner skills, and other factors. You must reach at least 65 points to lodge an Expression of Interest for most skilled visas.
faqs.preview_text: Overview of how age, English, work experience and education affect your Australian points score.

faqs.title: Which English tests are accepted for Australian skilled visas?
faqs.question: Can I use IELTS, PTE, TOEFL or other exams for my Australian skilled visa application?
faqs.answer: Australia accepts several English language tests for skilled migration, including IELTS, PTE Academic, TOEFL iBT, OET and Cambridge C1 Advanced. Each visa subclass has minimum score requirements and validity rules.
faqs.preview_text: Learn which English test results you can submit for an Australian skilled visa.

faqs.title: What is the minimum income requirement for UK spouse visas?
faqs.question: How much do I need to earn to sponsor my spouse or partner under the UK family route?
faqs.answer: The UK spouse visa route requires the sponsor to meet a minimum income threshold, which can change over time. You may combine employment income, self-employment income, and certain non-employment sources. Check the latest Home Office guidance for current thresholds.
faqs.preview_text: Details on financial requirements for sponsoring a spouse or partner in the UK.

faqs.title: How can I prepare for a US visa interview?
faqs.question: What should I bring and how should I prepare for my US visa interview at the consulate?
faqs.answer: Arrive early with all required documents, including your DS-160 confirmation page, appointment letter, passport, photo if needed, and supporting evidence for your visa category. Answer questions honestly and clearly, and be prepared to explain your ties to your home country.
faqs.preview_text: Practical tips to help you feel confident and organized at your US visa interview.

faqs.title: How long can I stay in the Schengen Area with a tourist visa?
faqs.question: What is the maximum stay allowed on a Schengen short-stay visa?
faqs.answer: A standard Schengen tourist visa allows you to stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period across the Schengen Area. Some travelers plan shorter trips, such as under 30 days, but the visa itself is usually issued for up to 90 days of stay.
faqs.preview_text: Understand the 90/180-day rule that applies to Schengen short-stay visas.

faqs.title: What is an Expression of Interest for New Zealand Skilled Migrant?
faqs.question: How do I submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) under the New Zealand Skilled Migrant Category?
faqs.answer: An Expression of Interest is an online submission where you provide details of your age, qualifications, work experience, and English ability. If you meet or exceed the current points threshold, Immigration New Zealand may invite you to apply for residence.
faqs.preview_text: Basic steps for lodging an EOI under the Skilled Migrant Category in New Zealand.

faqs.title: How do I create an account on this website?
faqs.question: Where can I register and what information is required to create an account?
faqs.answer: Click the Sign Up or Register link at the top of any page. You will need a valid email address, your name, and a secure password. After confirming your email, you can save checklists, plans, and bookmarks.
faqs.preview_text: Simple steps to register and start saving your visa plans and checklists.

faqs.title: What payment methods are accepted for consultations and services?
faqs.question: Can I pay for consultations and service packages with my local currency or card?
faqs.answer: We accept major credit and debit cards as well as some local payment options in selected countries. Charges are typically processed in USD, but your bank may convert from your local currency.
faqs.preview_text: Information about how to pay for consultations and service packages.

pathway_plans.name: Canada Express Entry – Federal Skilled Worker
pathway_plans.description: Points-based pathway for skilled workers to obtain Canadian permanent residency through Express Entry.
steps: Check eligibility and calculate CRS score.
Complete language testing and educational credential assessment (ECA).
Create an Express Entry profile and enter the pool.
Receive an Invitation to Apply (ITA).
Submit electronic Application for Permanent Residence (eAPR).
Provide biometrics and undergo medical exams.
Receive final decision and submit passport if requested.
pathway_plans.documentation_overview: Core documents include passports, language test results, ECA report, proof of work experience, police certificates, medical exams, and proof of funds.

pathway_plans.name: New Zealand Skilled Migrant Category
pathway_plans.description: Points-based residence pathway for skilled workers who want to live and work in New Zealand long-term.
steps: Check eligibility and calculate points score.
Submit Expression of Interest (EOI).
Wait for selection from the EOI pool.
Receive Invitation to Apply (ITA) for residence.
Submit residence application with supporting documents.
Complete health and character checks.
Receive residence decision and, if approved, receive resident visa.
pathway_plans.documentation_overview: Typical documents include passports, English language test results, qualifications assessments, employment references, and health and character documents.

pathway_plans.name: Australia General Skilled Migration
pathway_plans.description: Includes key skilled visas such as subclasses 189, 190, and 491 for qualified workers.
steps: Confirm occupation on the relevant skilled occupation list.
Complete skills assessment with the appropriate authority.
Sit an accepted English language test.
Submit Expression of Interest (EOI) via SkillSelect.
Receive invitation and lodge visa application.
Complete medical examinations and police checks.
Receive visa decision and arrange travel to Australia.
pathway_plans.documentation_overview: Skills assessment results, English test scores, employment references, qualifications, identity and character documents are all required.

pathway_plans.name: Australia Skilled Independent Visa (Subclass 189)
pathway_plans.description: Permanent visa for invited workers with skills needed in Australia, allowing them to live and work anywhere in the country.
steps: Confirm eligibility and occupation for subclass 189.
Obtain a positive skills assessment.
Achieve the required English language test scores.
Submit Expression of Interest via SkillSelect.
Receive invitation and lodge online application.
Provide biometrics, health exams, and police certificates.
Receive grant notice and travel to Australia.
pathway_plans.documentation_overview: Documentation should prove age, identity, skills assessment, work history, English proficiency, health, and character.

pathway_plans.name: UK Family Reunification – Spouse/Partner Route
pathway_plans.description: Allows eligible partners and children of UK residents to join them under the family route.
steps: Confirm relationship and financial eligibility.
Collect relationship, financial, and accommodation evidence.
Complete the online application and pay the visa fee and IHS surcharge.
Book and attend biometrics appointment.
Submit supporting documents to the visa application center or online.
Await decision and collect passport with visa vignette.
Travel to the UK and collect Biometric Residence Permit (BRP) if applicable.
pathway_plans.documentation_overview: Relationship evidence, income documents, accommodation details, tuberculosis test (if applicable), and English language test will be required.

pathway_plans.name: Canada Study Permit
pathway_plans.description: Temporary resident pathway for international students to study at designated learning institutions in Canada.
steps: Obtain a letter of acceptance from a designated learning institution.
Gather proof of financial support and ties to home country.
Complete the online study permit application and pay fees.
Provide biometrics and, if required, medical exams.
Wait for a decision and, if approved, receive a Port of Entry (POE) Letter of Introduction.
Travel to Canada and receive your study permit at the border.
pathway_plans.documentation_overview: Proof of acceptance, proof of funds, identity documents, travel history, and purpose of travel documentation are essential.

pathway_plans.name: US Professional Work Visas (H-1B and Similar Categories)
pathway_plans.description: Covers common US professional work visas where a US employer sponsors the foreign worker.
steps: Secure a qualifying job offer from a US employer.
Employer files required petition forms with USCIS and pays fees.
Wait for petition approval notice (Form I-797).
Complete DS-160 and pay consular visa fee.
Schedule and attend visa interview at a US embassy or consulate.
Receive visa decision and, if approved, receive visa in passport.
Enter the US and begin employment under the visa terms.
pathway_plans.documentation_overview: Employment offer letter, educational credentials, resume, petition approval notice, and supporting employer documents are required.

pathway_plans.name: Schengen Short-Stay Tourist Visa
pathway_plans.description: Short-stay visa for tourism or visiting friends and family in the Schengen Area.
steps: Determine which Schengen country is your main destination.
Make an appointment with the appropriate consulate or visa center.
Gather required documents including travel itinerary and proof of funds.
Submit application, pay visa fee, and provide biometrics.
Await processing and possible additional document requests.
Collect your passport with visa sticker if approved.
Travel to the Schengen Area and respect your authorized length of stay.
pathway_plans.documentation_overview: You must show confirmed travel plans, accommodation bookings, travel insurance, financial proof, and ties to your home country.

visa_guides.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Schengen Tourist Visas for Stays Under 30 Days
visa_guides.summary: A detailed walkthrough of the Schengen short-stay tourist visa process for trips of less than 30 days, from choosing the right consulate to preparing documents.
visa_guides.content: This guide explains who needs a Schengen visa, how to determine your main destination, and which consulate to apply at. It includes a document checklist, sample travel itineraries for trips under 30 days, and tips to avoid common refusal reasons. You will also find instructions on booking appointments, submitting biometrics, and understanding the 90/180-day rule.

visa_guides.title: Proof of Funds and Itinerary for Schengen Tourist Visas
visa_guides.summary: Guidance on how to document your travel plans and financial means for a short-stay Schengen tourist visa.
visa_guides.content: Consulates expect to see a realistic itinerary, including accommodation and onward travel, for the entire period you plan to spend in the Schengen Area. This guide explains acceptable proof of funds, how to show sponsorship, and how to avoid red flags such as last-minute bookings or incomplete accommodation coverage.

visa_guides.title: Canada Express Entry: Complete Overview for First-Time Applicants
visa_guides.summary: An end-to-end overview of the Express Entry system, including CRS scoring, draws, and document preparation.
visa_guides.content: In this guide, you will learn how Express Entry works, how to calculate your CRS score, and how to choose between Federal Skilled Worker, Federal Skilled Trades, and Canadian Experience Class. The guide covers recommended timelines, document strategy, and common pitfalls.

visa_guides.title: Reaching 65+ Points for Australian Skilled Visas
visa_guides.summary: Strategies to increase your points score for Australian skilled migration and qualify for invitation rounds.
visa_guides.content: This guide breaks down each category of the Australian points test and provides practical tips for boosting your score, such as improving English test results, gaining additional work experience, or studying in Australia.

visa_guides.title: How to Apply for a Canada Study Permit: Step-by-Step
visa_guides.summary: From getting an acceptance letter to submitting biometrics, this guide walks you through the Canada study permit process.
visa_guides.content: We cover how to choose a designated learning institution, prepare your Statement of Purpose, prove financial capacity, and avoid common refusal reasons.

visa_guides.title: US Work Visa Options for Skilled Professionals
visa_guides.summary: Overview of the most common US work visa categories and how to choose between them.
visa_guides.content: This guide explains eligibility and basic processes for H-1B, L-1, O-1 and other employment-based visas, including timelines and employer considerations.

visa_guides.title: Understanding the UK Family Visa Route for Spouses and Children
visa_guides.summary: A practical introduction to the UK family route, including eligibility, documents, and timelines.
visa_guides.content: We cover who can sponsor, income requirements, English language and accommodation rules, and what to expect after arrival in the UK.

visa_guides.title: New Zealand Skilled Migrant: From Expression of Interest to Residence
visa_guides.summary: Detailed roadmap covering each step of the New Zealand Skilled Migrant Category process.
visa_guides.content: This guide explains how points are calculated, how to lodge an EOI, what happens in selection rounds, and how to prepare a strong residence application.

visa_guides.title: Single vs Multi-Entry Schengen Tourist Visas Explained
visa_guides.summary: Learn the differences between single-entry and multi-entry Schengen tourist visas.
visa_guides.content: The guide explains how multi-entry validity works, when you might request it, and how it interacts with the 90/180-day rule.

visa_guides.title: Using Document Checklists to Stay Organized for Any Visa
visa_guides.summary: Tips on building and using document checklists so you never miss a requirement.
visa_guides.content: We show how to group documents, track expiry dates, and keep digital and physical copies synchronized for multiple visa processes.

document_template_items.name: Current Passport e Main Applicant
document_template_items.description: Clear color scan of the biographical page of your current valid passport.

document_template_items.name: Educational Certificates and Transcripts
document_template_items.description: Degree certificates and full academic transcripts for your post-secondary education.

document_template_items.name: Work Experience Reference Letters
document_template_items.description: Employer reference letters detailing your job title, duties, hours and dates of employment.

document_template_items.name: English Language Test Results
document_template_items.description: Official score report for an accepted English test such as IELTS, PTE Academic or TOEFL iBT.

document_template_items.name: Police Clearance Certificates
document_template_items.description: Police certificates from each country where you have lived for 12 months or more in the last 10 years.

document_template_items.name: Positive Skills Assessment
document_template_items.description: Official skills assessment outcome from the relevant Australian assessing authority for your occupation.

document_template_items.name: Birth Certificate
document_template_items.description: Full birth certificate showing your name, date of birth and parent details.

document_template_items.name: Current Passport e Principal Applicant
document_template_items.description: Scan of the photo page and any pages with visas or stamps.

document_template_items.name: Approved Language Test Results
document_template_items.description: Valid IELTS General Training or CELPIP score report used for Express Entry.

document_template_items.name: Educational Credential Assessment (ECA)
document_template_items.description: ECA report from a designated organization confirming your foreign education as equivalent to Canadian standards.

document_template_items.name: Proof of Settlement Funds
document_template_items.description: Bank statements or official letters showing sufficient available funds for settlement in Canada.

document_template_items.name: Passports for Sponsor and All Applicants
document_template_items.description: Valid passports for the UK sponsor, spouse/partner and each dependent child.

document_template_items.name: Relationship Evidence
document_template_items.description: Marriage certificate and additional evidence such as photos, joint bills or tenancy agreements.

document_template_items.name: Financial Requirement Evidence
document_template_items.description: Payslips, employment letter and bank statements to demonstrate that the income threshold is met.

document_template_items.name: Letter of Acceptance from DLI
document_template_items.description: Official letter of acceptance from a Canadian designated learning institution.

document_template_items.name: Study Plan / Statement of Purpose
document_template_items.description: Personal statement explaining your study plans, career goals and reasons for choosing Canada.

document_template_items.name: US Job Offer Letter
document_template_items.description: Signed employment offer letter from the sponsoring US employer, detailing role, salary and conditions.

document_template_items.name: Detailed Resume / CV
document_template_items.description: Up-to-date resume summarizing your education, work experience and key skills relevant to the petition.

document_template_items.name: Round-Trip Travel Itinerary
document_template_items.description: Flight reservations and a day-by-day itinerary for your stay of under 30 days in the Schengen Area.

document_template_items.name: Proof of Funds for Short Stay
document_template_items.description: Recent bank statements or sponsorship letter showing sufficient funds for accommodation, transport and daily expenses.
